action.reasoning: Observation: WiSE-FT sweep shows best alpha=0.90 with val worst=0.5915, basically same as alpha=1.00 (0.5903). The visual backbone interpolation didn't help much because we have a fine-tuned classifier head trained from scratch — interpolating only the visual backbone with original CLIP gives mismatched features. Test best so far is 0.4659 worst-region. Analysis: Need a different strategy. Options: (1) train longer with more epochs/better schedule, (2) train at higher resolution (336), (3) ensemble with a second seed, (4) FLYP-style contrastive fine-tuning that preserves CLIP zero-shot structure. With ~7.8h left and 21 iterations, I can afford another 1.5h training run. Let me train CLIP-L/14 longer (20 epochs) with stronger augs and lower LR, possibly continuing from current best ckpt. Decision: Resume from current best, train 10 more epochs with lower LR (3e-6), and add cutmix-like or stronger augmentation. Will use existing region-balanced setup (alpha 0.5 partial balance).
action.command: cd /workspace && RAB_ITERATION=10 torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train.py --epochs 8 --batch_size 32 --lr 3e-6 --wd 0.1 --clip_name openai/clip-vit-large-patch14 --out checkpoints/model_v2.pt --workers 6 --region_balance 0.5 --resume checkpoints/model.pt
